commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=78d91256470afe29086329332c588f79ea67fc2d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds LiteLLM Claude model detection to Anthropic-family cache retention and OpenAI-completions cache_control compatibility, plus focused helper tests.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +79. Total +101 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main keeps LiteLLM on openai-completions while both cache-retention family detection and cache_control payload compatibility omit that route. I did not run a live LiteLLM proxy reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer or contributor needs to supply real LiteLLM proof or an explicit proof override, and the docs finding should be addressed; ClawSweeper repair cannot replace the contributor's live environment evidence.

target.title: fix(llm): honor cacheRetention for LiteLLM-proxied Anthropic models
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #37966 — `cacheRetention` configured for LiteLLM-proxied Anthropic models (e.g. `litellm/claude-opus-4-6`) is silently ignored.

## Root Cause

Two code paths block LiteLLM from using Anthropic prompt caching:

1. **`resolveAnthropicCacheRetentionFamily()`** only recognizes direct Anthropic, Bedrock, and `anthropic-messages`-API providers. LiteLLM uses the `openai-completions` API path, so it falls through to `undefined`.

2. **`detectCompat()`** in openai-completions only sets `cacheControlFormat = "anthropic"` for OpenRouter, not LiteLLM. Without this, `cache_control` markers are never injected into the payload.

A previous PR (#38221) was closed because it only addressed part 1.

## Changes

### `anthropic-family-cache-semantics.ts`
- Added `isLiteLLMAnthropicModel()` helper — matches model IDs containing `claude` or starting with `anthropic/`
- Extended `resolveAnthropicCacheRetentionFamily()` with a LiteLLM clause: when `provider === "litellm"` + explicit cache config + Claude model ID → returns `"custom-anthropic-api"`

### `openai-completions.ts`
- Extended `detectCompat()` to set `cacheControlFormat = "anthropic"` for LiteLLM + Anthropic models (alongside existing OpenRouter check)

### Tests (`anthropic-family-cache-semantics.test.ts` — new file)
- `isLiteLLMAnthropicModel`: positive cases (claude, anthropic/ prefix) + negative cases (gpt-4, gemini, mistral)
- `resolveAnthropicCacheRetentionFamily` for LiteLLM: explicit cache config honored, non-claude rejected, no-config rejected
- Regression: existing anthropic-direct and OpenRouter paths unaffected

## Why This Works

LiteLLM natively passes through `cache_control` fields to Anthropic, so payload-level injection (same pattern as OpenRouter) is sufficient. No LiteLLM-specific SDK changes needed.## Real behavior proof

**behavior:** LiteLLM-proxied Anthropic models (e.g. `litellm/claude-opus-4-6`) now honor `cacheRetention` config by injecting `cache_control` markers into the payload, matching the existing OpenRouter integration pattern.

**environment:** Development workstation, Node.js v24, vitest test runner. No live LiteLLM proxy available for end-to-end verification.

**steps:**
1. Ran `npx vitest run src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/anthropic-family-cache-semantics.test.ts`
2. Traced both blocking code paths end-to-end: `resolveCacheRetention()` → `resolveAnthropicCacheRetentionFamily()` now returns `"custom-anthropic-api"` for LiteLLM+Claude with explicit config
3. Verified `detectCompat()` now sets `cacheControlFormat = "anthropic"` for LiteLLM+Claude models
4. Ran TypeScript compilation against `tsconfig.core.json` with 0 errors

**evidence:**
```
✓ unit anthropic-family-cache-semantics.test.ts (9 tests) 47ms
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  9 passed (9)
```
TypeScript compilation: 0 errors. The fix follows the exact same pattern as the battle-tested OpenRouter cache control integration. The original issue reporter (#37966) confirmed the fix works with a local monkey-patch of the same logic.

**observedResult:** All 9 unit tests pass, verifying: LiteLLM+Claude with explicit cache config returns `"custom-anthropic-api"` family; non-Claude models rejected; no-config case rejected; existing anthropic-direct and OpenRouter paths unaffected.

**notTested:** End-to-end verification with a live LiteLLM proxy instance. A maintainer with a LiteLLM setup can verify and apply `proof: override` if appropriate.
